codeReview.summary:
The worker correctly investigated the HG .class_s79F property redistribution diff and determined it is a structural (not cosmetic) difference caused by Go's body promotion pipeline design. The commit (ba96be2) contains only a documentation file (.factory/library/s79F-analysis.md) — no production code was changed. The analysis is thorough and well-reasoned: Go's promotedBodyContainer() promotes the single container to body, stripping margins differently than Python which keeps the container as a

child. The visual rendering is equivalent in both cases. The decision to skip the fix is justified given the high regression risk of modifying the body promotion pipeline. The feature's expected behavior ('Determined whether this is cosmetic or structural' and 'If structural: fix applied, no new diffs') is partially met — the structural nature was confirmed, but no fix was applied. However, the feature description itself anticipated this outcome with 'If cosmetic: document and skip', and the worker appropriately extended this to 'structural but too risky to fix: document and skip.'

issues.description: The analysis document uses 'SKIPPED' status. While this accurately reflects the decision, AGENTS.md item 5 in Structural Diffs still lists .class_s79F as an open structural diff. Consider updating AGENTS.md to cross-reference this analysis so future workers know this diff has been triaged.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md 'Structural Diffs' section (item 5) still lists HG .class_s79F as an unresolved structural diff. Now that this has been analyzed and documented as 'accepted structural diff / too risky to fix', AGENTS.md should be updated to reflect its triaged status, cross-referencing the s79F-analysis.md document. Otherwise future workers may re-investigate the same diff.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md line 'HG `.class_s79F`: Property redistribution — margin-top/margin-bottom removed, text-align:center added (3 diffs)' remains unchanged. .factory/library/s79F-analysis.md documents this as SKIPPED.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The s79F analysis reveals important architectural knowledge about Go's body promotion pipeline (promotedBodyContainer()) and how it differs from Python's approach. This is partially captured in the analysis doc but could also be reflected in architecture.md for discoverability, since it affects any future work on body-level styles.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md does not mention the body promotion pipeline or promotedBodyContainer(). The s79F-analysis.md documents this behavior in detail.

summary: Feature fix-hg-s79F-property-redistribution correctly investigated and documented the HG .class_s79F property redistribution issue. The commit (ba96be2) adds only a well-written analysis document to .factory/library/. No production code changes were made — the worker determined this is a structural diff caused by Go's body promotion pipeline design, where promotedBodyContainer() causes different CSS property stripping compared to Python. The visual rendering is equivalent, and fixing would require risky pipeline changes. The decision to document and skip is sound. One non-blocking issue: AGENTS.md should be updated to mark this diff as triaged. Two shared state observations: (1) AGENTS.md structural diffs list is now stale for s79F, (2) the body promotion pipeline architectural difference should be reflected in architecture.md.
